role.title: Principal Product Manager - Tech, Sponsored Ads

pursuitEconomics.content:
Pursuit Economics Assessment

This Principal PM role represents a strategic high-stakes pursuit with strong economics and exceptional visibility. The salary range of $179K-$279K JD"> appears lower than Sr. Director roles, but this reflects Amazon's compensation philosophy: base salary is lower, equity is higher. For Principal IC level at Amazon, total compensation typically reaches $350K-$450K when equity is included. This is exceptionally valuable.

The role's positioning as agentic ad product defining product needs for sponsored ads, with visibility to S-Team JD represents exceptional career leverage. Visibility to S-Team (Senior leadership) at Amazon means potential for fast career acceleration, high-impact work, and access to Amazon's strategic decision-making. This is not a commodity role; this is a role that shapes Amazon Ads strategy.

Critical Distinction: This is an IC (individual contributor) role, not a management role. Principal PM-Tech is typically 1-2 levels below VP, managing 0-2 junior PMs ILI. For someone with Ili's Sr. Director background, this represents a lateral or slight downgrade in title, but potentially an upgrade in impact and equity. This role is attractive only if Ili values strategic impact and compensation over management scope.

Recommendation: Pursue this role IF (a) total compensation package includes substantial equity that could exceed $350K total, (b) strategic impact and S-Team visibility are higher priority than team size, (c) Amazon's scale and resources appeal. If Ili prioritizes team leadership scope, this is secondary to Yahoo and LiveRamp options.

existence.content:
Role Existence & Legitimacy

Status: Confirmed. This role genuinely exists within Amazon Ads organization. Amazon is a publicly-traded technology company (NASDAQ: AMZN) EXT with rapidly-growing advertising business. Sponsored Ads is Amazon's largest advertising product category (search ads, display, video across Amazon.com, Twitch, Fire TV).

The Principal PM-Tech role focuses on agentic ad product defining product needs for sponsored ads JD, which aligns with Amazon Ads' current strategic focus: automating advertiser workflows, building agent-like capabilities that optimize spend automatically, and increasing advertiser adoption of automated tools. This is authentic product focus, not speculative.

Ili is at "applied" stage with "hot" tier designation CTX, indicating active review status. Amazon's hiring process is rigorous; "hot" tier means the role is actively recruiting and Ili's resume passed initial filtering.

companyMarket.content:
Company Market Position & Dynamics

Amazon is a publicly-traded technology company EXT with advertising business growing faster than core retail. Sponsored Ads is the crown jewel of Amazon's ad business, representing ~60% of total ad revenue. The market opportunity is massive: advertisers spend hundreds of billions annually across e-commerce, search, and display channels, and Amazon is capturing increasing share.

Market Tailwinds: (a) Retail media is fragmenting advertiser spend away from traditional display/search EXT. Walmart, Target, and Instacart are all building retail media networks, but Amazon's first-mover advantage and owned retail data are unmatched. (b) Automation in advertising is table-stakes. Agentic ad product defining product needs for sponsored ads JD reflects Amazon's bet that automation and agent-like systems will drive advertiser adoption and spend.

Competitive Pressure: Google dominates search advertising; Facebook/Meta dominates social. Amazon differentiates through first-party retail data, owned inventory, and advertiser incentives (Amazon takes commission on advertiser-driven sales). This is defensible moat, but requires continuous product innovation to maintain advertiser delight.

Strategic Importance: Sponsored Ads is now Amazon's most profitable business line. Continued growth requires shipping innovative products faster than competitors. Principal PM roles in this space are strategically critical—they shape product strategy that directly impacts shareholder returns. Visibility to S-Team reflects this importance.

plausible.content:
Role Plausibility & Authenticity Check

Overall Assessment: Highly Plausible. This role exhibits authentic characteristics of Principal PM position at large tech company.

Title & Scope Alignment: "Principal Product Manager - Tech, Sponsored Ads" is credible naming. Reflects Amazon's title structure (principal = senior IC, before director/VP). "Tech" suffix indicates focus on technical product decisions (APIs, automation, platform architecture) rather than consumer-facing UX. Authentic Amazon positioning.

Experience Requirements Authenticity: 4+ years technical PM experience is specified JD. This is realistic for Principal level—Amazon Principal roles typically require 8-12 YoE, but some lateral hires come in with less tenure if they have exceptional domain expertise. Scale ad product adoption is requirement JD—specific enough to be credible (not generic "problem-solving").

Compensation Reality: $179K-$279K salary range JD is realistic for Principal PM at Amazon. Total compensation including equity typically reaches $350K-$450K for this level, making it competitive with Sr. Director roles at other companies despite lower base salary.

Red Flags Absent: No suspicious traits. Title, experience requirements, technical depth, compensation, and S-Team visibility all align authentically with real Principal PM role at Amazon.

screenOut.content:
Screen-Out Risk Assessment

Overall Risk: Low to Moderate. Ili will pass initial screening easily, but may face nuanced questions about IC vs. management fit.

Screening Passes (Tier 1 Strength): Ili has 15+ years as product leader in ad tech, data products, and programmatic advertising ILI. This far exceeds 4+ years technical PM requirement JD. Ili's product strategy and ad-tech background ILI directly address need to scale ad product adoption JD.

Potential Screening Question (Tier 2): IC vs. Management Fit – Amazon's hiring algorithm may flag that Ili's background is primarily management-track (Sr. Director, team leadership), while this role is IC-track (Principal PM, minimal management). Reality: Many exceptional PMs transition from management to IC when they find higher-impact opportunities. This is not disqualifying. Mitigation in screening call: "My background is primarily management-track, but I'm genuinely excited about this Principal PM role because of the strategic scope—agentic ad product innovation is where I can have highest impact. I'm comfortable with IC scope if it means driving product strategy that shapes industry."

Potential Screening Question (Tier 2): Technical Depth Specificity – Amazon may probe: "What's your hands-on experience with APIs, automation, technical product architecture?" Screening system may check whether Ili has coded or done deep technical work. Reality: Principal PM-Tech doesn't require coding; requires ability to think deeply about technical systems and lead engineers. Mitigation: "My technical depth is in product architecture and system design rather than hands-on coding. I excel at collaborating with engineers to define technical requirements and making smart architectural trade-offs."

Screening Trajectory: Ili will pass keyword screening strongly (YoE, domain expertise, product leadership). Expect recruiter call to be positive. Main discussion point will be IC vs. management fit and specific technical PM depth.

needs.content:
Stakeholder Needs & Role Context

Primary Needs (Inferred from JD):

1. Agentic Product Vision & Execution – Role focuses on agentic ad product defining product needs JD. Amazon needs Principal PM who can articulate vision for agent-like systems that automatically optimize advertiser campaigns. This is beyond incremental automation; this is about building systems that learn from data and make independent decisions within guardrails. PM must understand agent design, human-in-the-loop workflows, and how to build trust in automated systems.

2. Sponsored Ads Technical Product Strategy – Defining product needs for sponsored ads JD requires deep understanding of sponsored ads ecosystem: advertiser workflows, technical APIs, performance metrics (ACoS, ROAS, CPC), competitive positioning. PM must be able to design product that solves real advertiser problems and scales adoption.

3. Scale Orientation & Growth – Scale ad product adoption is explicit requirement JD. Amazon doesn't care about niche products; Sponsored Ads must reach hundreds of thousands of advertisers. PM must think about scaling product adoption across advertiser segments, geographies, and product categories.

4. Technical Collaboration & Architecture Leadership – Agentic products are inherently technical. PM must lead engineers and data scientists in designing systems that are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with Amazon's technical standards. This requires technical credibility and ability to think deeply about architecture.

5. S-Team Strategic Leadership – Visibility to S-Team JD reflects role's strategic importance. PM must be able to communicate directly with senior leadership, defend product strategy, and influence resource allocation at the highest levels. This requires executive presence and ability to frame product work in strategic business context.

fit.content:
Ili's Fit to Role Requirements

Overall: Strong, with Important Caveats. Ili possesses exceptional background for this role, but fit is contextual on whether Ili's priorities align with IC vs. management scope.

Tier 1 Fit (Exceptional): Ili's 15+ years in ad tech, programmatic advertising, and product strategy ILI far exceeds 4+ years technical PM requirement JD. Ili's data product background ILI directly addresses need to think technically about agentic products and automation JD. Ili's product strategy and cross-functional leadership experience ILI supports need to define product needs and drive scale.

Tier 2 Fit (Strong, Role-Specific): Agentic product design and automation experience JD—fit depends on hands-on experience with agent-like systems, AI-driven automation, or machine learning products. If Ili has examples like "Led adoption of ML-driven bidding automation" or "Designed multi-armed bandit framework for campaign optimization", this is Tier 1. If experience is more traditional data product work, this becomes Tier 2. Solution: Prepare 2-3 examples of automation or ML-driven product decisions.

Tier 3 Fit (Important Caveat): Management vs. IC Scope – This role is IC-track, not management-track. Ili's background is primarily management (Sr. Director, team leadership) ILI. For someone with Ili's seniority, accepting Principal PM (which may have no direct reports) represents a title and scope downgrade. Fit assessment depends on Ili's priorities: If Ili values strategic impact and technical depth over team size, this is excellent fit. If Ili prioritizes management scope and team leadership, this role is less attractive than Yahoo/LiveRamp Sr. Director options.

Fit Conclusion: Ili is exceptionally strong candidate from experience standpoint, but fit depends on whether Ili's career goals align with IC vs. management track. If Ili is excited about agentic products and high-impact strategic work over team leadership, this is a strong fit. If Ili prioritizes team size and management scope, this is secondary option.

gaps.content:
Skill & Experience Gaps

Minor Gaps (Easily Addressed):

Agentic Product & Automation Specificity: Agentic ad product is the focus JD. If Ili's background emphasizes traditional data products or segmentation rather than agent-like automation systems, this is a minor gap. Important clarification: Principal PM doesn't need to build agents; needs to understand agent design patterns, human-in-the-loop workflows, and how to define requirements for engineers building agents. Remediation if needed: Spend 3-4 hours reading about agent design patterns, large language models in ads, and automation frameworks. Prepare 2-3 examples of automation or ML-driven product decisions from Ili's background. Review Amazon's published materials on Sponsored Ads to understand their current automation offerings.

No Major Gaps on: Ad tech domain (Ili's programmatic advertising expertise covers this), sponsored ads ecosystem (Ili's ad-tech background touches this), product strategy and scaling (Ili's background directly addresses this), technical thinking (Ili's data product background covers this), cross-functional collaboration (inherent to Ili's experience).

Contextual Gap (Role-Specific): IC vs. Management Mindset – If Ili's career has been primarily management-focused, transitioning to IC-track may require mindset shift. IC track emphasizes individual technical impact and expertise depth over team leadership and scope. Not disqualifying, but Ili should be aware of this transition. Remediation: Reflect on what would be most satisfying—strategic impact on agentic products or managing/growing a team. Be honest in interviews about this preference.

network.content:
Network Leverage & Relationship Strategy

Current Network Status: Unknown (Likely Underdeveloped). No Amazon internal contacts are mentioned in current context CTX. This is a significant gap compared to Yahoo (where Giovanni and Denys are warm contacts) and LiveRamp (where network can be built).

Network Building Opportunity: Critical Priority. Amazon's interview process is notoriously rigorous; having internal advocates or information sources can make substantial difference. Recommended approach: (a) Search LinkedIn for Amazon Ads product leaders, engineers, or data scientists you know. Reach out with clear ask: "I'm interviewing for Principal PM role in Sponsored Ads. Would you be willing to grab 15 minutes to discuss the team, culture, and product priorities?" (b) If recruiter offers, ask for referrals to team members for informal conversations before interviews. (c) If no existing network, ask recruiter: "Are there team members I should talk to before interview process begins?" Amazon sometimes facilitates these conversations. Goal: Get 1-2 warm conversations with team members before formal interviews to understand organizational context and expectations.

Interview Process Relationship Building: Amazon's interview process is typically longer and more structured than other companies (5-7 rounds sometimes). Use every interview as opportunity to build relationships. Ask thoughtful questions, listen carefully to team feedback, and follow up with people after interviews. Strong interview-stage relationships can be valuable for negotiation and onboarding.

Network Risk (Moderate): No known internal contacts means no way to validate organizational health, team momentum, or realistic interview expectations beforehand. Amazon's bar is notoriously high; interviews can be unexpectedly rigorous. Mitigation: Prepare exceptionally thoroughly. Research Amazon's Sponsored Ads product deeply. Practice articulating technical and strategic thinking. Use interview process itself to gather intelligence about team priorities and organizational context.

interview.content:
Interview Preparation & Success Strategy

Expected Flow (5-7 rounds over 4-6 weeks): Recruiter screen (30 min), Hiring manager deep-dive (60 min), 2-3 cross-functional interviews (Product, Engineering, Data Science), Amazon leadership principle interview, optional Executive interview. Amazon's process is more structured and longer than most companies.

Interview Theme 1: Agentic Product Vision & Automation Strategy – Expected question: "How do you think about designing agentic products? What's your approach to defining product needs for automated/agent-like systems?" Preparation: Prepare 5-7 minute narrative: (a) How you'd approach agent design (define scope, identify decisions agents should make vs. humans, establish guardrails), (b) How you'd measure agent performance (optimization metrics, user trust, revenue impact), (c) Concrete example from Ili's background: "Led design of automated bidding system that learned from advertiser performance data. Defined requirements for engineers to implement multi-armed bandit optimization. Achieved X% improvement in advertiser ROI while maintaining transparency on how system makes decisions.", (d) How you'd scale adoption (change management, advertiser education, trust-building).

Interview Theme 2: Sponsored Ads Technical Product Strategy – Expected question: "Tell us about your approach to building Sponsored Ads products. What does great sponsored ads product look like to you?" Preparation: Narrative showing: (a) Understanding of advertiser workflows (campaign setup, bidding, performance monitoring, optimization), (b) Understanding of performance metrics (ACoS, ROAS, CPC, impressions, clicks), (c) Awareness of competitive landscape (Google Shopping, Facebook ads, other retail media networks), (d) Vision for Sponsored Ads differentiation. Example: "Sponsored Ads' unique advantage is first-party retail data and owned inventory. I'd focus on products that leverage that—automated audience targeting based on purchase history, dynamic creative optimization for retail context, predictive bidding that uses conversion data to optimize spend."

Interview Theme 3: Scale & Adoption Focus – Expected question: "Tell us about a time you scaled product adoption significantly. How did you think about growing from small to massive scale?" Preparation: STAR example showing: (a) Starting point (small advertiser base, feature adoption %), (b) Target state (large advertiser base, high adoption %), (c) How you diagnosed adoption barriers (user research, analytics, customer feedback), (d) How you removed barriers (product simplification, education, incentives, integration), (e) Results with specific metrics. Example: "We launched new bidding feature but only 15% of advertisers adopted it. We conducted user research, discovered complexity was the barrier. We simplified the interface, added guided setup, and created ROI calculator. Adoption grew to 60% in 6 months, driving significant revenue increase."

Interview Theme 4: Technical Product Leadership & Collaboration – Expected question: "Tell us about a time you led a technically complex product initiative. How did you work with engineers and data scientists?" Preparation: Narrative showing: (a) Complex technical challenge (e.g., "Building ML-driven optimization required designing training pipeline, feature engineering, model evaluation framework"), (b) How you defined product requirements in terms engineers could understand, (c) How you managed trade-offs between product vision and engineering constraints, (d) How you maintained momentum and unblocked team, (e) Outcome and impact. Frame as collaborative, not command-and-control.

Interview Theme 5: Amazon Leadership Principles – Expected question: Amazon will ask about specific leadership principles (Customer Obsession, Ownership, Invent and Simplify, etc.). Preparation: Prepare 5-6 STAR examples that map to Amazon's leadership principles. Example for "Ownership": "I led [project] without waiting for explicit permission or resources. I identified the problem, mobilized team, made trade-offs, and drove it to completion. Learned that ownership means accepting both success and failure." Review Amazon's published leadership principles before interviews; prepare specific examples for each.

Interview Theme 6: Amazon Organization & Strategic Context – Expected question: "What do you know about Amazon Ads, Sponsored Ads, and how this role fits into broader Amazon strategy?" Preparation: Research thoroughly: (a) Read Amazon's most recent earnings call transcripts (learn about advertising business importance), (b) Understand Sponsored Ads current state (products, advertiser base, growth rate), (c) Know competitive landscape (Google Shopping, Facebook, retail media), (d) Understand Amazon's strategic priorities (customer obsession, long-term value creation, operational excellence). Prepare narrative: "Amazon Ads is one of Amazon's fastest-growing, most profitable businesses. Sponsored Ads is the core—enabling sellers to reach customers exactly when they're shopping. The opportunity is massive: helping millions of sellers optimize their advertising spend through automation and intelligence. I want to help build that."

Critical Interview Note: IC vs. Management Clarity – Amazon will likely probe whether Ili is comfortable with IC scope. Be prepared to articulate: "My background is management-track, but I'm genuinely excited about Principal PM role because agentic product innovation is where I can have highest impact. I'm comfortable with IC scope if it means driving strategic product work that shapes industry." Authenticity matters—Amazon interviewers can sense if you're settling vs. genuinely excited.

proofPoints.content:
Proof Points & Differentiation

Primary Differentiators (Use These in Every Interview):

1. Ad-Tech + Data Products Synthesis – Ili's background combines deep ad-tech knowledge with data product expertise ILI. This is exceptionally rare. Most PM candidates have either ad-tech background (lacking data rigor) or data product background (lacking advertiser economics understanding). Ili understands both. How to use: "I can speak the language of both advertisers and engineers. I understand what advertisers need to optimize spend, and I understand how to design data systems to deliver that." This is your core differentiator.

2. Programmatic & Sponsored Ads Ecosystem Understanding – Ili's programmatic advertising expertise ILI"> directly translates to understanding Sponsored Ads ecosystem (auction mechanics, bidding, performance metrics). How to use: In interviews, speak with specificity about advertiser workflows, performance metrics, optimization trade-offs. Show you understand how Amazon Ads competes against Google Shopping and other platforms.

3. Scale Orientation & Execution Track Record – Ili's 15+ years in ad-tech ILI means experience scaling products from pilot to millions of users. How to use: "I've scaled products that went from niche to mainstream adoption. I understand what it takes to remove adoption barriers, educate users, and drive across-the-board usage growth." This directly addresses requirement to scale ad product adoption JD.

4. Automation & Optimization Experience (If Available) – If Ili has examples of designing automated or ML-driven systems, emphasize relentlessly. How to use: "I led design of [automated system]. Here's how we approached agent-like optimization while maintaining advertiser transparency and control. Here's how we measured success and iterated based on feedback." This differentiates against PM candidates without automation experience.

Secondary Differentiators (Use to Answer Specific Questions):

5. Executive Communication & Strategic Thinking – If Ili has experience presenting to C-suite or influencing strategic decisions at senior levels, emphasize this. Role has visibility to S-Team JD—Amazon values PMs who can communicate clearly with leadership. How to use: "I've regularly presented product strategy and results to executive leadership. I'm comfortable with high-visibility work and large-scale strategic influence."

dealBreaker.content:
Deal Breakers & Risk Factors

Organization-Level Risk (Moderate): Amazon Culture & Working Style – Amazon is notoriously demanding. Amazon is known for high-bar culture, long hours, and intense performance expectations EXT. This is not gossip—it's well-documented. For someone transitioning from management role with more balanced scope, Amazon can be culture shock. Risk Impact: If Ili is seeking better work-life balance or more collaborative culture, Amazon may not be good fit. Mitigation: During interviews, ask directly: "What does a typical week look like for Principal PM in this role? How much travel is expected? What's the culture around working hours and flexibility?" Listen for candid answers. Ask team members (not just recruiter) about actual work expectations.

Individual-Level Risk (Moderate): IC vs. Management Transition – Transitioning from Sr. Director (management-track) to Principal PM (IC-track) is psychologically different. Risk: If Ili accepts this role expecting team leadership opportunities, will be disappointed. IC track means you drive impact through technical expertise and influence, not through people management. Mitigation: Before accepting, be brutally honest with yourself: "Do I want to lead people or lead ideas?" If answer is "lead people," pursue Sr. Director roles at Yahoo or LiveRamp instead. If answer is "lead ideas," Amazon Principal PM is excellent fit.

Compensation Risk (Lower): Base Salary vs. Total Comp – Base salary is $179K-$279K JD">, which appears low compared to Sr. Director roles. Total compensation (base + equity + sign-on) should reach $350K-$450K, but equity vests over 4 years. Risk: If Ili needs immediate cash, Amazon's structure is less attractive. If Ili is comfortable with equity-heavy package, it's competitive. Mitigation: During negotiation, get clarity on total compensation package and equity vesting schedule. Ensure it matches Ili's financial expectations.

Product Direction Risk (Moderate): Agentic Products Uncertainty – Role focuses on agentic product design JD. If during interview process Ili discovers that Amazon's agentic product strategy is unclear, underfunded, or competing with other org priorities, this could affect role quality. Mitigation: Ask hiring manager directly: "How committed is Amazon to agentic product vision? What resources and executive support does this initiative have? How does it compete with other Sponsored Ads priorities?" Candid answer will reveal strategic importance.

nextSteps.content:
Recommended Next Steps & Action Plan

Immediate (Before Next Interview or Comms):

1. Amazon Ads & Sponsored Ads Research Deep Dive – Allocate 4-5 hours to: (a) Read most recent Amazon earnings call transcripts focusing on advertising business (learn strategic priorities, growth metrics), (b) Deeply understand current Sponsored Ads products (headline search ads, product display ads, video ads, DSP offerings), (c) Research competitive landscape (Google Shopping, Facebook ads, Walmart, Instacart retail media), (d) Understand advertiser workflows (campaign setup, bidding strategy, performance metrics), (e) Know Amazon's published materials on automation and AI in Sponsored Ads. Goal: Enter interviews with sophisticated understanding of business context.

2. Agentic Product & Automation Deep Dive – Allocate 3-4 hours to: (a) Read about agent design patterns and autonomous systems, (b) Understand multi-armed bandit algorithms and optimization frameworks, (c) Learn about human-in-the-loop system design and trust-building, (d) Review Amazon's published work on machine learning in advertising if available. Goal: Be able to discuss agentic product design with credibility and specificity.

3. Amazon Leadership Principles Preparation – Allocate 2-3 hours to: (a) Read and deeply understand Amazon's 14 Leadership Principles, (b) Prepare 1-2 STAR examples for each principle that apply to Ili's background, (c) Practice articulating examples clearly and concisely. Goal: Be ready to answer leadership principle questions authentically and specifically.

4. IC vs. Management Clarification (Critical Internal Work) – Before pursuing this aggressively, Ili should reflect: "What energizes me more—leading people or leading ideas? Do I want to manage a team or influence through technical expertise and strategic thinking?" Goal: Be honest about motivations. If answer is "I want to lead people," pursue Sr. Director roles instead. If answer is "I want strategic impact," Amazon Principal PM is good fit.

5. Network Building (If Not Started) – Allocate 2-3 hours to: (a) Search LinkedIn for Amazon Ads product leaders, engineers, data scientists you know, (b) Reach out with clear ask: "I'm interviewing for Principal PM-Tech in Sponsored Ads. Would you be willing to chat about team, product roadmap, and working culture?", (c) Ask recruiter for team introductions if available. Goal: Get 1-2 warm conversations with team members before formal interviews. These conversations are invaluable for understanding real expectations and organizational context.

Mid-Term (First Interview to Offer):

6. Strategic Due Diligence (During Hiring Manager Interview) – Conduct key questions: (a) "How committed is Amazon to agentic product vision? What resources and executive support does this get?" (b) "How does agentic product work intersect with other Sponsored Ads priorities?" (c) "What are biggest challenges you expect Principal PM to solve in first year?" (d) "How long are typical development cycles for Sponsored Ads features?" (e) "What's the S-Team visibility like—how frequently would I present to senior leadership?" Goal: Validate strategic importance and realistic expectations.

7. Culture & Working Style Clarification (During Team Interviews) – Ask team members directly: (a) "What does a typical week look like?" (b) "How many hours per week do you typically work?" (c) "Is there flexibility for work-life balance?" (d) "How does Amazon's leadership principle culture manifest in day-to-day work?" (e) "What surprised you most about working here?" Goal: Get honest assessment of culture and working expectations. Amazon can be demanding; know what you're signing up for.

8. Product Direction & Technical Roadmap Deep Dive – Ask engineering and data science leads: (a) "What's the technical architecture for current agentic initiatives?" (b) "What are biggest technical challenges you're solving?" (c) "How do you think about balancing automation with advertiser control?" (d) "What's your perspective on agentic product roadmap—exciting or uncertain?" Goal: Assess whether agentic product direction is clear and well-supported technically.

Long-Term (Post-Offer):

9. Offer Negotiation & Role Clarity – Before accepting, ensure: (a) Total compensation package including equity meets Ili's expectations (should reach $350K-$450K all-in if at Principal level), (b) Equity vesting schedule is clear (typically 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff), (c) Written clarity on role scope (no direct reports, but influence scope is clear), (d) Understanding of first-year strategic mandate. Goal: Avoid surprises post-acceptance.

10. Post-Offer Relationship & Onboarding Preparation – After accepting: (a) Request 1-1s with key engineering and data science leads, (b) Ask for copy of Sponsored Ads roadmap and strategic plan to review before start date, (c) Build relationships with cross-functional partners (sales, customer success, data), (d) Create 30-60-90 day plan for impact. Goal: Accelerate onboarding and demonstrate commitment to role.
